description:
You are the site administrator in charge of making sure that every non-privileged user account has the correct time-zone (TZ) and language locale (LANG) settings.  
Two accounts already exist on the system:

• charlie – needs TZ “Asia/Kolkata” and LANG “en_IN.UTF-8”  
• diana  – needs TZ “America/Los_Angeles” and LANG “en_US.UTF-8”

Both users log in through the Bash shell, so their personal shell start-up file must be edited.  
When you are finished, create a single, comma-separated summary log that an automated checker can read.

Detailed requirements

1. Edit EACH user’s ~/.bashrc file so that the following two lines are present (exact spelling, no extra spaces):  
   export TZ="…correct value…"  
   export LANG="…correct value…"  

   • The two new export lines can appear anywhere in the file, but they must be on their own lines.  
   • Do NOT remove any pre-existing content if it is already there; simply add or update the two environment variables.  

2. Create the file /home/user/site_admin/time_locale_summary.log containing exactly the three lines shown below (including the header row and the final newline). The automated grader is case- and whitespace-sensitive, so be precise.  

username,TZ,LANG  
charlie,Asia/Kolkata,en_IN.UTF-8  
diana,America/Los_Angeles,en_US.UTF-8  

3. Ownership & permissions: you do NOT need to change file permissions. Every directory you have to write into is already writable by you.

4. Verification guidance for yourself (what the grader will check):  
   • grep -q '^export TZ="Asia/Kolkata"$' /home/user/charlie/.bashrc  
   • grep -q '^export LANG="en_IN.UTF-8"$' /home/user/charlie/.bashrc  
   • grep -q '^export TZ="America/Los_Angeles"$' /home/user/diana/.bashrc  
   • grep -q '^export LANG="en_US.UTF-8"$' /home/user/diana/.bashrc  
   • diff -q /home/user/site_admin/time_locale_summary.log <(printf "username,TZ,LANG\\ncharlie,Asia/Kolkata,en_IN.UTF-8\\ndiana,America/Los_Angeles,en_US.UTF-8\\n")  

To succeed, perform only the minimal number of shell commands you think are necessary (the whole job typically takes three or four simple commands).  
When you’re done, make sure the summary log content and the .bashrc modifications exactly match the specification above, character-for-character.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem layout (already in place when the agent starts):

/home/user/charlie/             – directory, writable  
/home/user/charlie/.bashrc      – text file, may be empty or contain unrelated lines  
/home/user/diana/               – directory, writable  
/home/user/diana/.bashrc        – text file, may be empty or contain unrelated lines  
/home/user/site_admin/          – directory, writable  

Ground-truth expectations AFTER the agent finishes:

1. /home/user/charlie/.bashrc must contain BOTH exact lines:
   export TZ="Asia/Kolkata"
   export LANG="en_IN.UTF-8"

2. /home/user/diana/.bashrc must contain BOTH exact lines:
   export TZ="America/Los_Angeles"
   export LANG="en_US.UTF-8"

3. /home/user/site_admin/time_locale_summary.log must exist with EXACT contents (including final newline):
username,TZ,LANG
charlie,Asia/Kolkata,en_IN.UTF-8
diana,America/Los_Angeles,en_US.UTF-8

4. No other files are required; no system-wide files (/etc/*) should be touched.
